meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 4)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Replace nouns with correct singular and plural pronouns in context.

questions.question: Which pronoun is a plural pronoun?
questions.explanation: "they" is a plural pronoun — it replaces a group of people or things.

questions.question: "It" is used to replace an animal or thing.
questions.explanation: Yes! "it" is used to replace a thing or animal when gender is not specified.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: "me" is the object (after the verb/preposition). The lion roars at me.

questions.question: The letter 'i' in the pronoun can sometimes be lowercase.
questions.explanation: The pronoun "I" is ALWAYS written as a capital letter. It is never "i".

questions.question: Meet Abigail and Ian. ___ both wear glasses.
questions.explanation: Abigail and Ian = two people → we use "They".

questions.question: Fill in: ___ see an old basket. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — it comes before the verb. I see an old basket.

questions.question: Carly wears a hat. ___ is red.
questions.explanation: "the hat" is a thing — we replace it with "It".

questions.question: Sort these pronouns: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: she/it/he = one person or thing (singular). they/we = more than one (plural).

questions.question: In the sentence "Mommy and I put our clothes in it," the pronoun 'I' is used correctly.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Mommy and I' is the subject of the sentence — 'I' is correctly used before the verb.

questions.question: The apples are yummy! → ___ are yummy! (one word)
questions.explanation: "The apples" is plural — use "They" to replace it.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Do you go to school by bus?" 'you' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: My sister and I like going to the park. → ___ like going to the park.
questions.explanation: "My sister and I" = two people (including the speaker) → use "We".

questions.question: "The boys are playing soccer. They are playing soccer." The pronoun 'They' is correct here.
questions.explanation: Yes! "The boys" is plural — we correctly replace it with "They".

questions.question: The dog barks loudly. → ___ barks loudly.
questions.explanation: "The dog" is an animal — we use "It" to replace it.

questions.question: Fill in: The lion roars at ___. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "me" is the object — it comes after prepositions like 'at'. The lion roars at me.

questions.question: Match each noun to the correct pronoun:
questions.explanation: She = female. They = multiple people. He = male. It = animal/thing.

questions.question: Which sentence uses pronouns correctly?
questions.explanation: "I am" is correct — 'I' is the subject before the verb.

questions.question: In the sentence "She always helps I with homework," the pronoun is correct.
questions.explanation: "helps I" is wrong. After a verb, use 'me': "She always helps me with homework."

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I am eight years old." 'I' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: Which sentence has the WRONG pronoun?
options: The dog eats food. It eats food.
Olivia is a friend. She is a friend.
My dad cooks dinner. He cooks dinner.
The children play outside. He plays outside.
questions.explanation: "The children" is plural — the correct pronoun is "They", not "He".
